_meta.name: Material Takeoff — Quantity Knowledge Base
_meta.description: Maps a project scope + size to an order-ready material quantity list. Same engine pattern as House Intelligence (a dataset of rules + a lookup engine), pointed at a different question: House Intelligence says what to INSPECT, Material Takeoff says what to BUY. Quantities already include standard waste factors, and the engine also reports the raw measurement + the waste % so the math is transparent and auditable. v1 covers ONE project type: kitchen_remodel.
_meta.disclaimer: This is an order-ready STARTING POINT computed from typical estimating standards, NOT a substitute for field measurement. Cabinets and countertops are MADE-TO-MEASURE: always field-verify before ordering those. Verify all quantities against the actual jobsite before purchasing.
principles: No waste, no shortage: waste factors are applied so you do not run short; coverage rates are deliberately conservative (e.g. thinset 75 sqft/bag, not 90+) so the order does not fall short.
Show the math: every line carries the raw measurement, the waste % (or coverage rate), and the final order quantity so the number is auditable.
Made-to-measure items (cabinets, countertops) carry no blind waste factor and must be field-verified.
sources: Drywall: 4x8 sheet = 32 sqft; waste 10% standard / 15% kitchens (cutouts). Joint compound ~25-30 lb per 100 sqft (Level 4). Tape ~2-3 sqft per linear ft.
Tile waste: 5-7% straight-lay, 10-15% diagonal, 15-20% mosaic/herringbone. One dye lot.
Cabinets: linear feet, made-to-measure (no waste factor), field-verify. 200 sqft kitchen ~= 35-45 LF total (~60% base / ~40% upper); 100 sqft / 10x10 ~= 20 LF.
Countertop: ~1.0 sqft finished per LF of base run; raw slab +15% (solid) to +25% (veined / pattern-matched).
Thinset: ~75-80 sqft per 50 lb bag (1/4 x 1/4 trowel).
Grout: ~100 sqft per 25 lb bag (12in tile, 1/8in joints); smaller tile / mosaic uses more.

kitchen_remodel.label: Full Kitchen Remodel
kitchen_remodel.summary: Quantified order list for a full kitchen remodel: cabinets, countertop, backsplash + floor tile and setting materials, drywall, and a plumbing / electrical rough-in checklist.

inputs.name: kitchenSqft
inputs.description: Kitchen FLOOR area in square feet. Drives every derived quantity.

inputs.name: ceilingHeight
inputs.description: Wall height, for drywall wall-area calculation.

inputs.name: cabinetLF
inputs.description: Known TOTAL cabinet linear feet (base + upper). If omitted, derived from floor area. If given, split 60% base / 40% upper unless baseCabinetLF / upperCabinetLF are also given.

inputs.name: baseCabinetLF
inputs.description: Known base-cabinet linear feet. Overrides the base split. Also drives countertop and backsplash if those are not given.

inputs.name: upperCabinetLF
inputs.description: Known upper-cabinet linear feet. Overrides the upper split. Drives under-cabinet lighting length.

inputs.name: countertopSqft
inputs.description: Known FINISHED countertop area. If omitted, derived as ~1.0 sqft per LF of base run.

inputs.name: tileLayout
inputs.description: Tile layout for backsplash + floor. Sets the tile waste %: straight 7%, diagonal 15%, herringbone 20%, mosaic 20%.

inputs.name: floorTile
inputs.description: Whether the floor is being tiled. Drives floor tile, and contributes to thinset + grout. Set false for LVP / wood / existing floor.

inputs.name: backsplashHeight
inputs.description: Backsplash height between counter and uppers. 18in is standard; use 0 to skip backsplash.

inputs.name: openings
inputs.description: Number of door/window openings, deducted from drywall wall area (~15 sqft each).

inputs.name: wallPerimeterLF
inputs.description: Known wall perimeter. If omitted, estimated as 4 * sqrt(floor area) (square-room approximation).

inputs.name: includeCeiling
inputs.description: Add the ceiling (= floor area) to the drywall quantity. Default false (most remodels keep the ceiling).

geometry.perimeter_note: Square-room approximation; override with wallPerimeterLF when the real perimeter is known (galley / L-shaped rooms have more wall).
geometry.cabinet_lf_per_sqft_note: Calibrated to: 100 sqft -> 20 LF total, 200 sqft -> 40 LF total. Scales with FLOOR AREA (not perimeter) so larger kitchens are not under-counted.

cabinets.note: Linear feet, made-to-measure -> NO waste factor. Always field-verify before ordering.

countertop.note: Raw = finished sqft (~1.0 per base LF). Slab order adds 15% (solid) to 25% (veined) cutting waste. Field-verify.

tile.waste_note: Top of each sourced band, chosen for the no-shortage promise (straight 5-7%, diagonal 10-15%, mosaic/herringbone 15-20%). Order from ONE dye lot.

thinset.note: ~75-80 sqft per 50 lb bag at 1/4 x 1/4 trowel. We use 75 (low end) so the order does not fall short. Applied to the substrate area being set (backsplash + floor).

grout.note: ~100 sqft per 25 lb bag for ~12in tile / 1/8in joints. Mosaic / small tile uses roughly double the grout, so coverage drops to ~50 sqft/bag for the 'mosaic' layout.

drywall.note: 4x8 sheet = 32 sqft. 15% waste for kitchens (cutouts) per standard. Wall area = perimeter * ceiling height, minus openings.

joint_compound.note: ~25-30 lb per 100 sqft (Level 4 finish); we use 30 (high end). Sold as ~4.5-gal (~61.7 lb) ready-mix buckets.

drywall_tape.note: Tape covers ~2-3 sqft per linear ft; we use 2 (more tape) so seams are covered. Sold in 250 ft rolls.

drywall_screws.note: ~32 screws per 4x8 sheet (16in field / 8-12in edges). A 1 lb box of 1-1/4in coarse screws holds ~350.

plumbing.note: Field-verify single vs. double bowl and cabinet fit.
Includes deck plate if required by the sink.
Braided stainless, sized to the shutoffs.
One hot, one cold under the sink.
Air gap or high loop per local code.
Confirm disposal vs. plain basket strainer; size the branch drain.

electrical.note: NEC: every counter >=12in gets a receptacle and no point along the counter is >24in from one. All counter receptacles GFCI-protected. (~1 per 4 LF of base run.)
NEC minimum two 20A small-appliance circuits serving the counter.

electrical.item: Dedicated appliance circuits (dishwasher, disposal, microwave, refrigerator)
electrical.note: One dedicated circuit each; confirm which appliances are present.
240V 40-50A for an electric range; omit if gas.
Size the run to the upper-cabinet linear feet; one switched/dimmed circuit.
Rough estimate at ~30 ft average run per circuit; size 12/2 (20A) and 14/2 (15A) per the actual circuit count and runs.
